primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder — CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Typical Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy was documented for only 8 weeks. Many BCBS policies for CPT 29827 require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of formal PT, but reviewers may scrutinize whether 8 weeks is sufficient given the severity of the tear and the short relief window.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add documentation clarifying that PT was maximally attempted and that the treating therapist concurred with surgical candidacy, or provide PT discharge summary confirming plateau of progress.

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection entry does not include a treatment duration or number of injections attempted. Payers may require documentation of at least one injection trial with a defined observation period before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Amend the chart to specify the observation period following the 11/05/2024 injection and confirm whether a second injection was considered or contraindicated.

denial_risk_flags.label: Conservative Care Dates Contain Placeholders
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Treatment dates for physical therapy, NSAIDs, and the corticosteroid injection reference placeholder tokens (October 2024, 11/05/2024) rather than resolved calendar dates. Payers require specific, verifiable treatment dates to confirm adequate conservative care duration.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Ensure all placeholder dates (October 2024, 11/05/2024) are replaced with actual calendar dates prior to submission to establish a clear and auditable conservative care timeline.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Date Contains Placeholder Token
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Both MRI and X-ray are dated 01/22/2025, which is a placeholder token. Payers require actual imaging dates to confirm the study was performed within an acceptable pre-authorization window.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 01/22/2025 with the actual imaging date before submission to ensure the imaging is recognized as current and valid for the authorization request.

diagnosis_codes.note: M75.121 (full-thickness rotator cuff tear, right shoulder) is a clinically appropriate and direct medical necessity indication for CPT 29827 arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: 2 of 2 duration-eligible treatments have an explicit numeric duration (100%). Meets the 50% threshold.

surgical_approach.note: The chart explicitly documents 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair' as the planned surgical approach, which is a specific and procedure-appropriate technique for CPT 29827.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 is a recognized orthopedic surgical code.
